Article.ArticleTitle: Long-lived photogenerated carriers enabled by S-scheme heterostructured HOF-on-hollow MOF core-shell nanoarchitectures for boosting self-powered photoelectrochemical sensor to detect enrofloxacin.

Abstract.AbstractText: Enrofloxacin (ENR) residues in foods and the environment accelerate the spread of antimicrobial resistance, yet routine trace-level surveillance remains hindered by matrix complexity and the cost limits of conventional assays. Here, we report a self-powered, dual-photoelectrode photoelectrochemical (PEC) aptasensor that couples a HOF-on-hollow ZIF-8 (HHZ-8) photocathode with an In2S3@CdIn2S4 photoanode to enable bias-free, ultrasensitive ENR detection. Specifically, the HHZ-8 core-shell structure features an S-scheme heterojunction between the HOF shell and hollow ZIF-8 core, whose synergistic nanoarchitecture enhances light harvesting and electron transfer while mitigating the HOF limitation of rapid charge recombination. This design extends charge carrier lifetimes by 6.8-fold and significantly enhances the PEC response. The aptasensor achieves ultrasensitive detection of ENR across a broad linear range (10.0 pM to 1.0 μM) with an ultralow detection limit (3.3 pM), high selectivity, and excellent stability. This work pioneers an S-scheme heterostructure bridging HOFs and hollow MOFs, providing a strategic pathway to engineer long-lived carriers for high-performance autonomous sensing platforms.
